texto: If you have to be careful because of oppression and censorship this pressure produces diamonds.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Tatyana Tolstaya sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “If you have to be careful because of oppression and censorship this pressure produces diamonds.” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, adversity, careful, útil em momentos de reflexao.

texto: Politics disappears it vanishes. What remains constant is human life. So I try to develop a perspective in my writing where politics is just one of the pieces of furniture in this furnished world. It is not the purpose. It is not the goal.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Tatyana Tolstaya sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Politics disappears it vanishes. What remains constant is human life. So I try to develop a persp…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, writing, world, útil em momentos de writing e world.

texto: I don't think people are fools, and I think they deserve a good attitude and smart entertainment.
explicacao: Citação de Tatyana Tolstaya sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I don't think people are fools, and I think they deserve a good attitude and smart entertainment.” articula reflexao, good, smart em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como smart e people.

texto: You read, move your lips, figure out the words, and it's like you're in two places at the same time: you're sitting or lying with your legs curled up, your hand groping in the bowl, but you can see different worlds, far-off worlds that maybe never existed but still seem real. You run or sail or race in a sleigh--you're running away from someone, or you yourself have decided to attack--your heart thumps, life flies by, and it's wondrous: you can live as many different lives as there are books to read.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tatyana Tolstaya: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “You read, move your lips, figure out the words, and it's like you're in two places at the same ti…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, books, fantasy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de fantasy e lives.

frase_original: You read, move your lips, figure out the words, and it's like you're in two places at the same time: you're sitting or lying with your legs curled up, your hand groping in the bowl, but you can see different worlds, far-off worlds that maybe never existed but still seem real. You run or sail or race in a sleigh--you're running away from someone, or you yourself have decided to attack--your heart thumps, life flies by, and it's wondrous: you can live as many different lives as there are books to read.
